mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of rapid unemployment growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high retail sales growth and low inflation indicate robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving corporate revenue expansion and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% YoY, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing activity and supply-side capacity utilization, which immediately boosts market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price index levels erode real corporate margins and force aggressive monetary tightening, creating a negative risk premium that drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 320, or the negative correlation breaks down during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth falls below 1.5% quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained macroeconomic growth that directly increases corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following GDPC1 increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and manufacturing slowdown, triggering short-term risk aversion that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during periods of stable inventory levels.
